sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Newly diagnosed ovarian cancer and related symptoms.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports severe pain and bleeding, prompting her physician to order tests. She also reports weight loss and constipation. She denies vomiting. She is 52 years old and reports having had her period at age 13 and menopause at age 28. She has been adopted and has no family history of gynecological cancers. She reports no prior history of endometriosis. She has no children. She currently takes no hormone replacement therapy.
Findings
Physical exam reveals slight tenderness to palpation of the left lower quadrant. An adnexal mass is palpated on the left side. The patient's CT scan shows a 3 cm left ovarian mass with associated lymph node involvement. There is no evidence of gross peritoneal or metastatic disease.
Assessment
The patient is being evaluated for ovarian cancer. Based on the CT scan, the stage is estimated to be stage III A. The patient will undergo genetic counseling and testing.
Plan
The doctor recommends initiating chemotherapy with cisplatin and taxol after surgery. A lymph node dissection and hysterectomy will be performed. The doctor recommends intraperitoneal chemotherapy. Genetic counseling and testing will be arranged. Follow-up will be scheduled in four weeks.
